activities_projects.title: Tracing Paper Twins
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of congruence through superposition
steps: Draw a triangle on a sheet of paper
Trace the exact same triangle onto tracing paper
Cut out the traced triangle and place it over the original to see if they cover each other exactly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that congruent figures are identical in shape and size

activities_projects.title: SSS Congruence Construction
steps: Construct a triangle with sides 4cm, 5cm, and 6cm
Construct a second triangle with the same measurements
Cut them out to verify they are congruent
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns the SSS condition for congruence
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of measurements and construction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: SAS Match-Up
steps: Draw two triangles with two sides of 5cm and 7cm
Ensure the included angle between these sides is 60 degrees for both
Measure the third side to see if they match
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies the SAS rule to identify congruent twins
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct measurement of the included angle (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: ASA Rule Explorer
steps: Draw a line segment of 6cm
Draw angles of 40 and 80 degrees at the endpoints
Complete the triangle and repeat the process to create its 'twin'
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct construction of angles and side (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: RHS Right-Angle Twins
steps: Draw two right-angled triangles
Make sure both have a hypotenuse of 10cm and one side of 6cm
Verify if the remaining side is equal in both
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of hypotenuse and right angle (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Real-World Congruence Hunt
activities_projects.objective: Identify congruent objects in the environment
steps: Find five pairs of 'geometric twins' in your house (e.g., biscuits, tiles, stamps)
List them and explain why they are congruent
Measure their dimensions to prove congruence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates mathematical congruence to real-life objects
activities_projects.evaluation: Diversity of examples and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Not-a-Twin' Challenge
steps: Draw two equilateral triangles, one with 3cm sides and one with 6cm sides
Explain why they have the same shape but are NOT congruent twins
Note down the difference in size
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between congruent and similar figures

activities_projects.title: Congruent Stamp Making
steps: Carve a simple triangle shape into a potato or eraser
Use it as a stamp to create a pattern on paper
Explain why every stamped image is a congruent twin of the others

activities_projects.title: Corresponding Parts Labeling
activities_projects.objective: Identify CPCT (Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles)
steps: Draw two congruent triangles ABC and PQR
Color corresponding sides with the same colors
List all 6 pairs of corresponding parts (3 sides, 3 angles)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Correctly identifies corresponding vertices and sides

activities_projects.title: Congruence Logic Quiz
steps: Create 5 'True or False' questions about congruence (e.g., 'Are all circles congruent?')
Swap the quiz with a classmate
Grade each other's work based on the rules learned
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Strengthens theoretical understanding of congruence rules
